   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "53cb7a10",
   "metadata": {

   "source": [
    "# Coupling Analysis: Multi-Channel Coupling\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/advanced_coupling.ipynb)\n",
    "\n",
    "This tutorial demonstrates the **Coupling Function (CF)** analysis workflow using `gwexpy.analysis.CouplingFunctionAnalysis`.\n",
    "\n",
    "## What is a Coupling Function?\n",
    "\n",
    "A coupling function quantifies how much a *witness* (environmental) sensor's noise couples into a *target* (sensitive) channel. It is defined as:\n",
    "\n",
    "$$\n",
    "\\mathrm{CF}(f) = \\sqrt{\\frac{P_{\\rm target,inj}(f) - P_{\\rm target,bkg}(f)}{P_{\\rm witness,inj}(f) - P_{\\rm witness,bkg}(f)}}\n",
    "$$\n",
    "\n",
    "where $P_{\\rm inj}$ and $P_{\\rm bkg}$ are the power spectral densities during injection and background periods.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Typical application:** PEM (Physical Environment Monitor) injection analysis in gravitational-wave detectors.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Legacy migration note:**  \n",
    "If your legacy code manually computed PSDs, divided them, and applied custom thresholds via scipy, this workflow replaces all of that.  \n",
    "See the [Migration Guide for GWpy Users](../gwexpy_for_gwpy_users_en.md) and [GWpy Difference API Index](../gwpy_added_api_index_en.md) for migration guidance."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "0fb025df",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.007147,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:52:51.585355+00:00",

     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:52:51.578208+00:00",

    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "> **Note — SigmaThreshold statistical validity**: When the number of averaged segments `n_avg` is below 10, the Gaussian approximation of the χ²(2K) distribution becomes less accurate. A `UserWarning` will be raised in this case. Consider using `PercentileThreshold` for short data segments."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "b62b32cb",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:52:51.627041Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:52:51.626751Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.037680Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.032689Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 9.425491,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.040128+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:52:51.614637+00:00",

   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from astropy import units as u\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.analysis.coupling import (\n",
    "    CouplingFunctionAnalysis,\n",
    "    RatioThreshold,\n",
    "    SigmaThreshold,\n",
    ")\n",
    "from gwexpy.timeseries import TimeSeries, TimeSeriesDict"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "6192d583",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.012047,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.072751+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.060704+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Prepare Synthetic Injection / Background Data\n",
    "\n",
    "We simulate:\n",
    "- A **witness** channel (environmental sensor): seismic or acoustic\n",
    "- Two **target** channels (sensitive instruments): one strongly coupled, one weakly coupled\n",
    "\n",
    "The injection adds a coherent tone to the witness. The targets respond proportionally to the coupling strength."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "29bd2c85",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.217447Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.216486Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.251457Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.247664Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.04532,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.252688+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.207368+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Background channels: ['ACC:FLOOR_X', 'GW:DARM', 'GW:AUX']\n",
      "Injection channels:  ['ACC:FLOOR_X', 'GW:DARM', 'GW:AUX']\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rng = np.random.default_rng(0)\n",
    "\n",
    "fs   = 512     # sample rate [Hz]\n",
    "T    = 32.0    # duration [s]\n",
    "n    = int(fs * T)\n",
    "t    = np.arange(n) / fs\n",
    "dt   = (1 / fs) * u.s\n",
    "\n",
    "# Frequency bins for injected tones\n",
    "f_inj1 = 12.5   # Hz\n",
    "f_inj2 = 25.0   # Hz\n",
    "\n",
    "# Noise floor\n",
    "noise_level = 0.1\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Background data (no injection) ---\n",
    "wit_bkg  = rng.normal(0, noise_level, n)\n",
    "tgt1_bkg = rng.normal(0, noise_level, n)\n",
    "tgt2_bkg = rng.normal(0, noise_level, n)\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Injection data: excite the witness with tones ---\n",
    "inj_signal = 5.0 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * f_inj1 * t) + 3.0 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * f_inj2 * t)\n",
    "\n",
    "wit_inj  = inj_signal + rng.normal(0, noise_level, n)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Target 1: strong coupling (CF ≈ 0.4 at f_inj1, 0.3 at f_inj2)\n",
    "cf_true1 = 0.4\n",
    "tgt1_inj = cf_true1 * inj_signal + rng.normal(0, noise_level, n)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Target 2: weak coupling (CF ≈ 0.05)\n",
    "cf_true2 = 0.05\n",
    "tgt2_inj = cf_true2 * inj_signal + rng.normal(0, noise_level, n)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Wrap in TimeSeries\n",
    "def make_ts(data, name, unit=u.ct):\n",
    "    return TimeSeries(data, dt=dt, t0=0*u.s, unit=unit, name=name)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Background dict\n",
    "data_bkg = TimeSeriesDict({\n",
    "    \"ACC:FLOOR_X\":  make_ts(wit_bkg,  \"ACC:FLOOR_X\",  u.m / u.s**2),\n",
    "    \"GW:DARM\":      make_ts(tgt1_bkg, \"GW:DARM\",      u.m),\n",
    "    \"GW:AUX\":       make_ts(tgt2_bkg, \"GW:AUX\",       u.m),\n",
    "})\n",
    "\n",
    "# Injection dict\n",
    "data_inj = TimeSeriesDict({\n",
    "    \"ACC:FLOOR_X\":  make_ts(wit_inj,  \"ACC:FLOOR_X\",  u.m / u.s**2),\n",
    "    \"GW:DARM\":      make_ts(tgt1_inj, \"GW:DARM\",      u.m),\n",
    "    \"GW:AUX\":       make_ts(tgt2_inj, \"GW:AUX\",       u.m),\n",
    "})\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Background channels:\", list(data_bkg.keys()))\n",
    "print(\"Injection channels: \", list(data_inj.keys()))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8897eee5",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.012417,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.279929+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.267512+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Run Coupling Function Analysis\n",
    "\n",
    "`CouplingFunctionAnalysis.compute()` takes the injection and background `TimeSeriesDict`, and the name of the witness channel."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "48d6b245",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.330425Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.330078Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.651191Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.649094Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.33567,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.653656+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.317986+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Results computed for: ['GW:DARM', 'GW:AUX']\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "cfa = CouplingFunctionAnalysis()\n",
    "\n",
    "results = cfa.compute(\n",
    "    data_inj=data_inj,\n",
    "    data_bkg=data_bkg,\n",
    "    fftlength=4.0,          # 4-second FFT segments\n",
    "    overlap=0.5,             # 50% overlap\n",
    "    witness=\"ACC:FLOOR_X\",  # the environmental sensor\n",
    "    threshold_witness=RatioThreshold(ratio=10.0),   # witness must show 10× excess\n",
    "    threshold_target=RatioThreshold(ratio=2.0),     # target must show 2× excess\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Results computed for:\", list(results.keys()))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3c495111",
   "metadata": {

     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.688658+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:01.667956+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. Inspect Results\n",
    "\n",
    "Each result is a `CouplingResult` containing the coupling function, upper limit, and diagnostic PSDs."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "fd7d7242",
   "metadata": {

      "CF valid bins: 6\n",

      "CF at f_inj2 bin: 0.401 (expected ~0.4)\n"
     ]
    },

   ],
   "source": [
    "# Strong coupling channel (GW:DARM)\n",
    "res_darm = results[\"GW:DARM\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"CF valid bins:\", res_darm.valid_mask.sum())\n",
    "print(\"CF at f_inj1 bin:\", end=\" \")\n",
    "\n",
    "freqs = res_darm.cf.frequencies.value\n",
    "idx1 = np.argmin(np.abs(freqs - f_inj1))\n",
    "idx2 = np.argmin(np.abs(freqs - f_inj2))\n",
    "print(f\"{res_darm.cf.value[idx1]:.3f} (expected ~{cf_true1})\")\n",
    "print(f\"CF at f_inj2 bin: {res_darm.cf.value[idx2]:.3f} (expected ~{cf_true1})\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Simple CF plot\n",
    "res_darm.plot_cf(xlim=(5, 100));"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "## 4. Threshold Strategies\n",
    "\n",
    "gwexpy provides three built-in threshold strategies:\n",
    "\n",
    "| Strategy | Description | Best for |\n",
    "|----------|-------------|----------|\n",
    "| `RatioThreshold(ratio)` | $P_{\\rm inj} > r \\cdot P_{\\rm bkg}$ | Fast screening, physical reasoning |\n",
    "| `SigmaThreshold(sigma, n_avg)` | Gaussian significance test | Stationary, Gaussian backgrounds |\n",
    "| `PercentileThreshold(pct, factor)` | Empirical distribution | Non-Gaussian, glitchy backgrounds |"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "c89af457",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:53:05.969016Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:53:05.968699Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:53:06.684317Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:53:06.682437Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.737863,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:06.688337+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:05.950474+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 800x400 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "with warnings.catch_warnings():\n",
    "    warnings.simplefilter('ignore')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Compare threshold strategies\n",
    "    n_avg = T / 4.0  # approximate averaging count for 4-second FFT\n",
    "\n",
    "    strategies = {\n",
    "    \"Ratio×10/2\":   (RatioThreshold(10.0), RatioThreshold(2.0)),\n",
    "    \"Sigma 3σ\":      (SigmaThreshold(3.0),  SigmaThreshold(2.0)),\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))\n",
    "\n",
    "    for label, (thr_w, thr_t) in strategies.items():\n",
    "        res = cfa.compute(\n",
    "        data_inj=data_inj,\n",
    "        data_bkg=data_bkg,\n",
    "        fftlength=4.0,\n",
    "        overlap=0.5,\n",
    "        witness=\"ACC:FLOOR_X\",\n",
    "        threshold_witness=thr_w,\n",
    "        threshold_target=thr_t,\n",
    "        )\n",
    "        cf = res[\"GW:DARM\"].cf\n",
    "        valid = ~np.isnan(cf.value)\n",
    "        ax.plot(cf.frequencies.value[valid], cf.value[valid], marker=\".\", label=label)\n",
    "\n",
    "        ax.axhline(cf_true1, color=\"gray\", linestyle=\"--\", label=f\"True CF={cf_true1}\")\n",
    "        ax.set_xscale(\"log\")\n",
    "        ax.set_yscale(\"log\")\n",
    "        ax.set_xlim(5, 100)\n",
    "        ax.set_xlabel(\"Frequency [Hz]\")\n",
    "        ax.set_ylabel(\"Coupling Function\")\n",
    "        ax.set_title(\"Threshold Strategy Comparison\")\n",
    "        ax.legend()\n",
    "        plt.tight_layout()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "40608e2f",

   "source": [
    "## 5. Frequency Range Restriction\n",
    "\n",
    "Use `frange` to restrict the coupling function evaluation to a specific frequency band."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,

       "<Plot size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "results_band = cfa.compute(\n",
    "    data_inj=data_inj,\n",
    "    data_bkg=data_bkg,\n",
    "    fftlength=4.0,\n",
    "    overlap=0.5,\n",
    "    witness=\"ACC:FLOOR_X\",\n",
    "    frange=(10.0, 50.0),   # only evaluate 10–50 Hz\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "cf_band = results_band[\"GW:DARM\"].cf\n",
    "valid_band = ~np.isnan(cf_band.value)\n",
    "print(f\"Valid CF bins in 10–50 Hz: {valid_band.sum()}\")\n",
    "results_band[\"GW:DARM\"].plot_cf(xlim=(5, 100));"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 6. Upper Limits\n",
    "\n",
    "At frequencies where the witness is excited but the target shows no measurable excess, gwexpy computes an **upper limit** on the coupling function.\n",
    "\n",
    "This is useful for setting constraints even when the coupling is below the noise floor."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "b968a736",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:53:07.612875Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:53:07.612563Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:53:08.129446Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:53:08.127915Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.537539,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:08.133605+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:53:07.596066+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Upper limit bins: 0\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Upper limit is automatically computed in cf_ul attribute\n",
    "res_darm = results[\"GW:DARM\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "cf_ul = res_darm.cf_ul\n",
    "ul_valid = ~np.isnan(cf_ul.value)\n",
    "print(f\"Upper limit bins: {ul_valid.sum()}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# The full diagnostic plot includes both CF and upper limit\n",
    "res_darm.plot_cf(xlim=(5, 100));"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5ab8a848",

     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Summary\n",
    "\n",
    "```python\n",
    "from gwexpy.analysis.coupling import CouplingFunctionAnalysis, RatioThreshold\n",
    "\n",
    "cfa = CouplingFunctionAnalysis()\n",
    "results = cfa.compute(\n",
    "    data_inj=data_inj,        # TimeSeriesDict with injection data\n",
    "    data_bkg=data_bkg,        # TimeSeriesDict with background data\n",
    "    fftlength=4.0,\n",
    "    witness=\"ACC:FLOOR_X\",\n",
    "    threshold_witness=RatioThreshold(25.0),\n",
    "    threshold_target=RatioThreshold(4.0),\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Access result for one target\n",
    "res = results[\"GW:DARM\"]\n",
    "res.plot()         # diagnostic 3-panel plot\n",
    "res.cf             # FrequencySeries: coupling function values\n",
    "res.cf_ul          # FrequencySeries: upper limits\n",
    "```\n",
    "\n",
    "**See also:**\n",
    "- [Advanced Bruco (Broadband Noise Analysis)](advanced_bruco.ipynb)\n",
    "- [Advanced Correlation Analysis](advanced_correlation.ipynb)"
   ]
  }
